description: Method violates the contract of JsonParser.nextToken() by throwing IOException instead of only JacksonException.
reasoning: The base class JsonParser defines nextToken() to throw JacksonException (a runtime exception). The implementation in UTF8DataInputJsonParser wraps this in a try-catch block for IOException and throws it, which forces callers to handle checked exceptions not specified in the base class contract, breaking substitution.

description: Method violates the contract of JsonParser.nextName() by throwing IOException instead of only JacksonException.
reasoning: Similar to nextToken(), nextName() is defined in the base class to throw only JacksonException. By wrapping the logic in a try-catch for IOException and rethrowing, the subclass forces callers to catch checked exceptions, which is incompatible with the expected behavior of the base class.

description: Method signature in subclass forces checked exception handling not present in base class.
reasoning: While the method is protected, the pattern of catching IOException and wrapping it into a JacksonException is inconsistent across parser implementations. In this specific case, the implementation relies on the base class behavior but forces a wrapper that alters the signature expectations of internal components.
